blocks.content: Lena listened. She didn't rush toward comfort or reassurance. She sat in the truth of what Cassandra had said and let it be the size it was.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Can I tell you what I actually think?

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: I think the version he loves is entirely real. I think you are the realest person I know, Cass, and the thing you're carrying is part of that realness — it's part of what made you who you are. The way you don't go past things. The way you notice everything. The way you stayed on that wall.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: He's not in love with an edited version of you. He's in love with the version that makes every day count, that sends songs at nine o'clock, that stood on a porch and said: I know, that's what makes it harder. That's not an edited version. That's all of you.
A quiet. The carnival went on around them.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I still have to tell him.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: I know. I'm not saying you don't.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: You don't have to say it perfectly, Cass. You just have to say it honestly. That's all it needs to be.
The words arrived simply. Without performance. Exactly as they needed to arrive.
